title: 10 best herbal teas for acid reflux
intro_desc: Herbal teas for acid reflux are caffeine-free, plant-based beverages that can help alleviate the symptoms of acid reflux by reducing stomach acid and inflammation. Examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for acid reflux include chamomile, ginger, licorice root, and marshmallow root tea. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract, while ginger tea can help reduce nausea and inflammation. Licorice root tea can help increase the mucus lining of the esophagus, protecting it from stomach acid. Marshmallow root tea can also help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals with acid reflux can find relief from their symptoms, allowing them to enjoy their meals and improve their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with acid reflux because its soothing properties calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms. The herbal tea's carminative effect helps to ease bloating and discomfort by gently relaxing the stomach muscles, allowing food to pass through more smoothly. Additionally, lemon balm's natural antacid properties help neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from heartburn and indigestion. By promoting a healthy gut environment, lemon balm teas can help alleviate acid reflux symptoms naturally.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm tea helps to relax the muscles in the esophagus and stomach, reducing spasms and inflammation that can contribute to acid reflux.

Carminative property: Lemon balm tea has natural carminative properties, which means it helps to dispel gas and reduce bloating, making it easier for digestive enzymes to break down food and reducing symptoms of acid reflux.

Antioxidant property: Lemon balm tea contains antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to inflammation and damage to the esophageal lining, exacerbating acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have a calming effect on the digestive system, making them ideal for soothing heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain oils that help relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers according to their availability.
Measure one teaspoon of dried lemon balm or two teaspoons of fresh lemon balm for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured lemon balm with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser and let it steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea leaves from the hot liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Pour the clear herbal tea into a cup and serve warm or chilled, sweetened with honey if desired, within an hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with acid reflux because it contains compounds that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by digestive acids. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm down the digestive tract and prevent excessive acid production, thereby alleviating heartburn and regurgitation symptoms associated with acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root's glycyrrhizin content has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds present in licorice tea have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by acid reflux.

Liquiritin: Liquiritin, a bioactive compound found in licorice root, has been shown to relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and reducing symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with acid reflux.
Bark: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which help to calm down inflammation and reduce symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried licorice root per cup of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes before straining.
Add honey or sugar to taste and stir well after removing from heat.
Pour into a teapot or mug and serve hot immediately after preparation.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with acid reflux because it contains a natural compound called anethole, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, fennel's soothing properties help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing stomach acids to move more smoothly through the system without irritating the esophageal lining. This gentle, non-invasive approach can provide relief from heartburn and other discomforts associated with acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Antacid properties: Fennel tea helps to neutralize stomach acid, reducing symptoms of heartburn and acidity.

Soothing effects on the digestive tract: Fennel's mucilaginous properties (its ability to form a protective film) help calm inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from acid reflux.
Carminative properties: Fennel tea helps eliminate gas and bloating from the digestive system, reducing pressure on the lower esophageal sphincter and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain fiber and antioxidants that help soothe digestive issues, including acid reflux.
Seeds: The seeds of the fennel plant have a calming effect on the stomach and can help reduce inflammation caused by acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried fennel leaves or seeds per eight ounces.
Combine the measured fennel with the boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes before straining.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or piece of cheesecloth.
Serve hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and enjoy within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common agrimony teas helps with acid reflux because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux. The tea's flavonoids and polyphenols also help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, the tea's demulcent properties coat and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, further reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties of Agrimonia tea may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms.

Antacid and soothing effects: The flavonoids and polyphenols present in Agrimonia tea have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to neutralize excess stomach acid and soothe the lining of the esophagus and stomach.

Gastroprotective properties: Agrimonia's gastroprotective compounds may help protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and duodenum from damage caused by stomach acid, reducing the risk of acid reflux and related complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their bitter properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Bark: The bark is used because it contains a compound called Agrimoniin, which has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh agrimonia eupatoria leaves and flowers at dawn or late afternoon when dew is still present, gathering approximately one ounce dried material.
Clean the harvested materials by gently rinsing them with cool running water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat dry with paper towels.
Combine one teaspoon of clean dried agrimonia eupatoria leaves and flowers with one cup boiling water in a large teapot or infuser, allowing steeping for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids, discarding the solids and retaining only the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving warm, enjoying its earthy flavor and potential health benefits, with a recommended consumption of 1-2 cups daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with acid reflux because it contains flavonoids and terpenes, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. The tea's flavonoids also help to strengthen the stomach lining, reducing the likelihood of acidic contents flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, pot marigold tea has been shown to inhibit the production of acid in the stomach, providing further relief for those experiencing frequent acid reflux episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, such as beta-carotene and lutein, can help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by stomach acid, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux episodes.
Mucilages: Pot marigold contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that can help soothe and coat the esophagus, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Flowers: The delicate petals contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant properties, which soothe and protect the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers or use store-bought dried petals for 2 tablespoons of tea.
Measure out 200ml of boiling water and pour it over the calendula in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flowers to infuse their flavor and medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable drinking temperature before serving and enjoying its subtle, floral flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime teas helps with acid reflux because of its unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. These compounds work together to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing the likelihood of stomach acid flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms. As a result, drinking large-leaved lime teas may provide natural relief from heartburn and other acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antacid properties: The tea's antacid properties help neutralize stomach acid, reducing symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux by balancing the body's pH levels.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Large-leaved lime tea's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with acid reflux.
Antioxidant properties: The tea's antioxidants help protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract from damage caused by acid reflux, reducing the risk of complications and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and alkaloids that help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.
Bark: Contains compounds that aid in digestion, neutralize stomach acids, and provide relief from heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves, selecting only those with no signs of disease or pests, for a yield of approximately 30 grams.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with cool water to remove any dirt, debris, and excess moisture.
Combine the cleaned leaves with one quart of boiling filtered water in a large glass infuser or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavonoids, tannins, and volatile oils to dissolve into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean pot, discarding the solids and serving the infusion hot or cold as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey teas helps with acid reflux because it contains compounds that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract. The allantoin and rosmarinic acid present in comfrey tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach lining. This, in turn, can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and chest pain, allowing for more efficient digestion and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Helps to soothe and heal digestive tract inflammation, reducing acid reflux symptoms by calming the mucous membranes and promoting healthy tissue regeneration.
Vitamin K: Supports the health of the digestive tract lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to acid reflux and helping to repair any damage caused by stomach acids.
Triterpenoid saponins: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and regurgitation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of comfrey contains mucilages that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and stems or use dried comfrey herb.
Measure one tablespoon of dried comfrey per cup of boiling water.
Steep the comfrey mixture for 10-15 minutes in a large tea infuser.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then add honey to taste if desired.
Drink immediately or store in an airtight container for up to two days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with acid reflux because it provides a soothing and protective coating for the esophagus and stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The mucilage in marshmallows forms a barrier that neutralizes stomach acid, preventing it from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing heartburn. This natural remedy also helps to calm down digestive enzymes, further easing symptoms of acid reflux and promoting a sense of relief for those who suffer from it.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow tea contains a thick, gel-like substance called mucilage, which coats and soothes the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux.
Alkaline properties: The herbal marshmallow tea has an alkaline effect on the body, helping to neutralize excess stomach acid and reduce symptoms of acid reflux, such as heartburn and bloating.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating discomfort and pain caused by acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and protect the digestive tract, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.

Seeds: Marshmallow seeds contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow root althaea officinalis in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Rinse dried or fresh marshmallow roots with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop 10-20 grams of dried or 50-100 grams of fresh marshmallow root into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Steep chopped root in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release flavonoids and mucilage properties.
Strain tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and discard solids before serving warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano teas helps with acid reflux because it contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antacid properties. The antioxidants present in oregano, such as carvacrol and thymol, help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating discomfort caused by acid reflux. Additionally, the tea's soothing effects can calm the digestive tract, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and reducing the production of stomach acids that contribute to acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Oregano tea contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, which can contribute to acid reflux.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol, a phenolic compound found in oregano, has been shown to have gastroprotective effects by reducing gastric acid secretion and inflammation, helping to alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Thymol: Thymol, another active compound in oregano, has antacid properties that help neutralize stomach acid and reduce the risk of acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Oregano roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to calm digestive issues, including acid reflux, due to their soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the oregano plant contain compounds like rosmarinic acid and carvacrol that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and relieve heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried oregano leaves from your garden or store-bought supply.
Measure one teaspoon of dried oregano or two teaspoons of fresh oregano per eight ounces water.
Boil one quart of filtered water in a large pot and add measured oregano.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes to release oils and flavors.
Strain tea into cups using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with acid reflux because of its natural antacid properties, which help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The herb's carnosic acid also exhibits anti-inflammatory effects, which can soothe and heal the lining of the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. Additionally, rosemary teas may help slow down digestion, allowing food to move through the system more efficiently and minimizing the risk of stomach contents flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Rosemary tea's high antioxidant content helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to acid reflux symptoms.

Muscle Relaxation and Spasm Prevention: Rosemary's essential oils, particularly camphor and bornyl acetate, have a calming effect on smooth muscle tissue, which can help relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES) and prevent gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

Digestive Bitters and Stimulant Properties: Rosemary tea's bitter compounds stimulate digestion and improve gastric emptying, helping to reduce symptoms of acid reflux by increasing stomach acid production and reducing the likelihood of food staying in the esophagus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rosemary leaves have a soothing effect on the digestive tract, helping to reduce inflammation and ease symptoms of acid reflux.
Roots: The roots of the rosemary plant contain compounds that help to neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosemary leaves measure one teaspoon for every eight ounces.
Combine the measured rosemary leaves with boiling water pour the water over leaves in.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to ten minutes then strain and discard solids.
Add honey or lemon juice to taste optional sweeteners and flavorings.
Pour the herbal tea into cups and serve immediately or store refrigerated.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat acid reflux with herbal teas is by choosing teas with natural anti-inflammatory and antacid properties. Look for chamomile, ginger, licorice root, and marshmallow root teas. Chamomile and licorice root soothe irritated esophageal tissues, while ginger and marshmallow root help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. To maximize benefits, drink these teas after meals and before bedtime, but avoid drinking large amounts right before lying down. Remember that while herbal teas can complement traditional treatments, they should not replace any prescribed medications or professional medical advice.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acid reflux that are treated with herbal teas include functional dyspepsia and gastritis. Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce irritation in the esophagus and stomach lining. Peppermint and ginger teas are also commonly used to alleviate symptoms of digestive discomfort, such as bloating, gas, and nausea. Licorice root tea can help increase mucus production in the gastrointestinal tract, protecting the mucous membrane from stomach acid. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies, especially for those with underlying medical conditions or taking medications.
